heroLede: I'm Koh — a former BIM & architectural coordinator moving into UX/UI design. Six years of making complex systems legible for other people; now doing it on screens instead of buildings.
name: Koh [Last Name]
aboutParagraphs: I spent the last several years coordinating MEP and architectural systems on large-scale hospital projects — reading drawings the way a UX designer reads a user flow, and fixing clashes the way a designer resolves friction points.

I'm currently completing Google's UX Design Certificate and building out a design practice from the ground up: user research, wireframing, prototyping in Figma, and usability testing. This site will grow alongside that work — right now it's a foundation, not a finished portfolio.

What I bring that's harder to teach: precision under complexity. Years of reconciling hundreds of data points into something a contractor could act on without ambiguity. That instinct transfers directly into design systems, information architecture, and documentation.

transferList.to: Reading complex systems → mapping user flows
